---
title: "Example document in PDF"
author: 
  - name: Prof. Saulo Chaves
    orcid: 0000-0002-0694-1798
    email: saulochaves@usp.br
    affiliation:
      - name: Department of Genetics, ESALQ-USP
        city: Piracicaba, São Paulo, Brazil
format: 
  pdf:
    toc: true
    bibliography: ../../Misc/references.bib
    embed-resources: true
---

Only once, install `tinytex` using 

```
quarto install tinytex
```


# Heading 1

## Heading 2

### Heading 3

#### Heading 4 

##### Heading 5

###### Heading 6

# Markdown syntax

*italics*, **bold**, ***bold italics***

superscript^2^ / subscript~2~

~~strikethrough~~

`verbatim code`

# Link & Images

<https://www.esalq.usp.br/pg/programas/genetica/>

[PPG Genética e Melhoramento de Plantas](https://www.esalq.usp.br/pg/programas/genetica/)

![Angry Bateson](../../Misc/bateson2.png)

[![Araújo et al.](../../Misc/araujo.png)](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41597-025-06190-3)


# Lists

* unordered list
  + sub-item 1
  + sub-item 2
    - sub-sub-item 1


*   item 2

    Continued (indent 4 spaces)


1. ordered list
2. item 2
   i) sub-item 1
      A.  sub-sub-item 1
      
      
1. ordered list
2. item 2

   ```r
   print("Hello, World!")
   ```

   A.  sub-sub-item 1


- [ ] Task 1
- [x] Task 2


term
: definition


# Tables

| Right | Left | Default | Center |
|------:|:-----|---------|:------:|
|   12  |  12  |    12   |    12  |
|  123  |  123 |   123   |   123  |
|    1  |    1 |     1   |     1  |

<https://www.tablesgenerator.com/>


# Source code

```{r}
a = c(1,2,3,4)
b = c(5,6,7,8)
c = c(9,10,11,12)
d = c(13,14,15,16)
mat2 = rbind(a,b,c,d)
mat2
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)

plot(penguins$bill_len, penguins$bill_dep)
```


# Source code

inline math: $P = G + E + G \times E$

display math:

$$
\sigma^2_P = \sigma^2_G + \sigma^2_E + \sigma^2_{G\times E}
$$

# Citations

-   Build a `.bib` file with proper formatting. For example:

```
@book{wickham2017r,
  title={R for data science},
  author={Wickham, Hadley and Grolemund, Garrett and others},
  volume={2},
  year={2017},
  publisher={O'Reilly Sebastopol}
}
```

Then cite @wickham2017r or [@wickham2017r]

# References
